name: Ministry of Education and Child Care

goals.short_name: Every child in B.C. can access a K-12 education that fits their family

main_services.name: Choose an independent or alternative school
Support your child's safety and wellbeing at school

goals.short_name: Students progress through school and graduate prepared for what's next

main_services.name: Graduate and plan your next step

goals.short_name: Families can access affordable, quality early learning and child care

main_services.name: Find and pay for child care
Access inclusive and Indigenous child care supports

goals.short_name: B.C.'s child care sector is safe, staffed and growing

main_services.name: Work as an early childhood educator
Operate a licensed child care facility

acts.summary: Governs B.C.'s public K-12 school system, including student enrolment, homeschool registration, online learning, standard educational programs, early learning programs, and the B.C. Certificate of Graduation and Adult Graduation Diploma.

sections.title: Eligibility to enrol in an educational program
sections.description: Sets out when a child is permitted to enrol in an educational program provided by a board.

services.name: Register your child for kindergarten or elementary school

sections.title: Online learning and online learning school definitions
sections.description: Defines online learning and online learning schools; a student ordinarily resident in B.C. may enrol with a Provincial or District Online Learning School.

services.name: Enrol in online learning for Kindergarten to Grade 12

sections.title: Registration of children educated at home
sections.description: A public school principal must register the child of any parent who wishes the child to be educated at home; the principal offers evaluation services and loan of resource materials free of charge.

services.name: Register your child as a homeschooler

sections.title: Standard educational program
sections.description: Defines the standard educational program, including programs provided according to an individual education plan for students with additional learning needs.

services.name: Get support for a child with additional learning needs
services.ms_name: Support your child's safety and wellbeing at school

sections.title: British Columbia Certificate of Graduation
sections.description: Authorizes the minister to issue the British Columbia Certificate of Graduation (Dogwood Diploma) to students who meet graduation requirements.

services.name: Meet B.C. graduation requirements for the Dogwood Diploma
services.ms_name: Graduate and plan your next step

sections.title: British Columbia Adult Graduation Diploma
sections.description: Authorizes the minister to issue the British Columbia Adult Graduation Diploma (Adult Dogwood) to eligible adult learners.

services.name: Get an Adult Graduation Diploma
services.ms_name: Graduate and plan your next step

sections.title: Early learning program definition
sections.description: Defines an early learning program as one for children who are less than school age, designed to improve readiness for kindergarten, that a parent or designated caregiver must attend with the child (the basis for StrongStart BC).

services.name: Attend a free StrongStart BC early learning program
services.ms_name: Find and pay for child care

acts.summary: Provides for the classification, certification and funding of independent schools in B.C., and for registration of homeschooled children with independent school authorities.

sections.title: Registration of home schooled children
sections.description: An independent school authority may register a homeschooled child and may set a registration fee; the school offers evaluation services and loan of resource materials free of charge.

services.name: Register your child as a homeschooler

sections.title: Classification of independent schools
sections.description: Sets out the Group 1-4 classification system and the requirements a prospective independent school authority must satisfy for certification.

services.name: Establish a new independent school
services.ms_name: Choose an independent or alternative school

sections.title: Grant; Special purpose grants
sections.description: Authorizes per-student operating grants and special purpose grants to certified independent school authorities.

services.name: Get funding as a certified independent school
services.ms_name: Choose an independent or alternative school

acts.summary: Establishes the Commissioner and Director of Certification, the B.C. Teachers' Council, and the certification framework required to teach in B.C. public and most independent schools.

sections.title: Certification and Letters of Permission
sections.description: Section 30 (Issuance of certificate) and section 35 (Issuance of letter of permission) set out the process for obtaining a certificate of qualification to teach in B.C.

services.name: Get certified to teach in B.C. schools
services.ms_name: Graduate and plan your next step

acts.full_name: Community Care and Assisted Living Act
acts.summary: Requires a licence to provide care to 3 or more children (a child care program) and governs inspection, compliance and enforcement of licensed community care facilities; detail is set out in the Child Care Licensing Regulation.

sections.title: Licensing of community care facilities
sections.description: Requires a person providing a prescribed child care program to hold a licence issued under the Act; regulation-making authority for the Child Care Licensing Regulation.

services.name: Open a licensed child care facility
services.ms_name: Operate a licensed child care facility

sections.title: Inspection, compliance and complaints
sections.description: Health authority licensing officers inspect licensed facilities and investigate complaints about the health, safety or well-being of children in care.

services.name: Make a complaint about a licensed child care facility
services.ms_name: Access inclusive and Indigenous child care supports

acts.citation: B.C. Reg. 332/2007 (under the Community Care and Assisted Living Act, S.B.C. 2002, c. 75)
acts.summary: Sets out facility, health, safety, staffing and early childhood educator qualification requirements for licensed child care programs, and exemptions such as StrongStart BC and school-age summer camps.

sections.title: Early childhood educator qualification requirements
sections.description: Requires staff providing care in a licensed child care facility to hold the applicable ECE Registry certification.

services.name: Get certified as an early childhood educator
services.ms_name: Work as an early childhood educator

sections.title: Facility, health, safety and staffing requirements
sections.description: Sets minimum requirements for facility design, ratios, health and safety that a licensee must meet to obtain and keep a child care licence.

services.name: Open a licensed child care facility
services.ms_name: Operate a licensed child care facility
